content:
{"cards": [{"name": "Goblin Sprinter", "mana_cost": "{1}{R}", "cmc": 2, "colors": ["R"], "color_identity": ["R"], "type_line": "Creature — Goblin Warrior", "oracle_text": "Haste
Menace", "flavor_text": ""Two legs good, four legs faster, but sharp pointy stick best of all!"", "power": "2", "toughness": "1", "loyalty": null, "rarity": "common", "design_notes": "An aggressive 2-mana red creature testing the statline ceiling for common. 2/1 haste for 2 mana is the baseline, and menace adds evasion without being too complex. Both keywords support red's aggressive strategy. The fragile toughness prevents it from dominating the board while still providing immediate pressure."}, {"name": "Pyroclasm Dragon", "mana_cost": "{5}{R}{R}", "cmc": 7, "colors": ["R"], "color_identity": ["R"], "type_line": "Creature — Dragon", "oracle_text": "Flying, haste
When ~ enters, it deals 3 damage to each creature without flying.
{2}{R}: ~ deals 2 damage to any target.", "flavor_text": "Where it flies, the sky itself burns.", "power": "6", "toughness": "4", "loyalty": null, "rarity": "mythic", "design_notes": "A splashy mythic dragon that embodies red's destructive power. The enters trigger is a one-sided pyroclasm effect, clearing non-flying blockers. The activated ability provides repeatable burn, making it a threat even when not attacking. At 7 mana, it's expensive enough to be fair while still being a game-ending threat. Multiple abilities create the complexity expected at mythic."}, {"name": "Nature's Guidance", "mana_cost": "{1}{G}", "cmc": 2, "colors": ["G"], "color_identity": ["G"], "type_line": "Sorcery", "oracle_text": "Search your library for a basic land card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle.", "flavor_text": "The forest whispers directions to those who know how to listen.", "power": null, "toughness": null, "loyalty": null, "rarity": "common", "design_notes": "A variant on land search that puts the land in hand rather than onto the battlefield. This prevents immediate mana acceleration but enables land drops and fixes mana. The reveal clause is standard templating for search effects that go to hand. Positioned as a budget alternative to Rampant Growth that trades immediate acceleration for hand size."}, {"name": "Ironbark Behemoth", "mana_cost": "{4}{G}{G}", "cmc": 6, "colors": ["G"], "color_identity": ["G"], "type_line": "Creature — Beast", "oracle_text": "Trample", "flavor_text": "Its footsteps shake the earth for miles around, announcing its arrival to friend and foe alike.", "power": "7", "toughness": "5", "loyalty": null, "rarity": "common", "design_notes": "Tests the upper limit for common creature stats. At 6 mana, a 7/5 with trample represents green's philosophy of large, efficient creatures. The double green in the cost ensures it's primarily for green decks. Trample is essential on large creatures to prevent chump blocking. The stats exceed the CMC+3 guideline but are justified by the high mana cost and single keyword simplicity."}, {"name": "Verdant Overgrowth", "mana_cost": "{2}{G}", "cmc": 3, "colors": ["G"], "color_identity": ["G"], "type_line": "Enchantment", "oracle_text": "At the beginning of your upkeep, you may search your library for a basic land card, put it onto the battlefield tapped, then shuffle.
Creatures you control with power 4 or greater have trample.", "flavor_text": null, "power": null, "toughness": null, "loyalty": null, "rarity": "uncommon", "design_notes": "A build-around enchantment that combines green's ramp and big creature themes. The upkeep trigger provides gradual mana acceleration, while the static ability rewards playing large creatures. The 'may' on the search prevents forced shuffling in unfavorable situations. The power 4 threshold creates interesting deckbuilding decisions. Two complementary abilities at uncommon complexity."}]}
